title: Friday Sermon 16 June 1995
description:
The Majlis e Shura of Spain is starting and I draw their attention to instructions given in previous sermons regarding Shura [consultation].  The Divine attributes spring from the 4 fundamental attributes mentioned in the Sura al-Fatiha.  There is not a single attribute which cannot be connected to these Divine attributes.  An example is that of the attribute al-Haq [The Truth] which pertains to various verses which speak on the subject of the causes of falsehood.  They are: false boasts, consuming what is prohibited, to partake of everything filthy for falsehood's sake etc.  These are all things related to covetousness.

Those who have accepted falsehood as their master in this world and take refuge in falsehood at the time of every tribulation, they take refuge in falsehood out of greed, they take refuge in falsehood whenever they seek praise, the whole of their lives both internal and external, are based upon a principle of falsehood.  It is a great lie if they deem that they will be rescued by the Truth on the Day of Judgement.  So without connecting to the Truth, one cannot succeed either in this world or in the hereafter.  We will have to emerge as a true Community and until we instigate truthfulness within every Ahmadi so that it becomes second nature that is to say one returns to one's primary nature we cannot, in reality succeed in this world or in the hereafter.

We shall proceed further in the following Sermon.  A very dedicated and special Life Devotee of ours Chaudrey Mushtaq Ahmad Bajwa (the former Missionary in-Charge of Switzerland) has passed away 'We belong to Allah and return to him'.  The deceased had numerous habits that would draw one's heart to him and the most interesting of those was truth.  He had been a cancer patient for a long time but lived in a way which was nothing short of a miracle.  At the time of death he was 83 years old.  The deceased had served as Imam and Missionary in-Charge for 3 years in England as well.


The text https://www.alislam.org/urdu/pdf/Khutbat-e-Tahir-Vol-14-1995.pdf#page=432
